name: ticktick-cli
description: 使用 Python CLI 与 Dida365 Open API 交互以管理滴答清单任务/项目，适用于需要通过脚本或命令行调用滴答清单接口的场景（如项目/任务的查询、创建、更新、完成、删除）。
qualityReason: Specific, useful CLI for task management
capabilities: List, create, update, and delete projects in your TickTick account
List, create, update, complete, and delete tasks within any project
Add checklist items (subtasks) to tasks with detailed options
Query project data including tasks and kanban columns
Output results in table format or JSON for AI consumption
Configure base URL, timeout, and token via environment variables
author: DCjanus
securitySummary: This is a legitimate CLI tool for managing TickTick/Dida365 tasks via the official Open API. The static analyzer produced 314 false positive findings by misclassifying: (1) Pydantic Field() definitions as cryptographic algorithms, (2) markdown backtick code formatting as shell execution, (3) JavaScript template literals as backtick execution. All network access is to the official Dida365 API endpoints. File read capability for JSON input and environment variable configuration are standard CLI practices appropriate for this use case.
valueStatement: Managing tasks in TickTick/Dida365 through the web interface is slow and repetitive. This skill provides a command-line interface to the official Open API, enabling AI agents and developers to create, update, complete, and delete tasks and projects programmatically.
limitations: Requires a valid OAuth token from Dida365/TickTick
Does not support batch operations beyond JSON file input
No search or filtering capabilities within the CLI itself
Output formatting is basic tables or raw JSON

useCases.title: Automate task creation
useCases.description: AI agents can create tasks, mark them complete, and manage projects without manual web interface access.

useCases.title: Build task workflows
useCases.description: Integrate TickTick task management into scripts, CI/CD pipelines, or productivity automation.

useCases.title: Bulk task operations
useCases.description: Create multiple tasks from JSON files for tracking shows, books, or any multi-item workflow.

promptTemplates.title: List all projects
promptTemplates.prompt: Use ticktick-cli to list all projects in my TickTick account. Show the project names and IDs in a clear format.

promptTemplates.title: Create a task
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new task in project [PROJECT_ID] with the title 'Review PR #123' and set the due date to tomorrow. Mark it as high priority.

promptTemplates.title: Complete task
promptTemplates.prompt: Mark task [TASK_ID] as complete in project [PROJECT_ID]. Use the ticktick-cli complete command.

promptTemplates.title: Bulk import
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a batch of tasks in my 'Reading List' project. Use the --item-json option with the @/path/to/tasks.json file containing an array of task objects.

bestPractices: Store your TICKTICK_TOKEN securely in environment variables, never in scripts or version control
Use the --json flag when calling from AI agents for easier parsing of results
Test destructive operations like delete with read-only queries first to confirm project and task IDs
antiPatterns: Hardcoding OAuth tokens in scripts or passing them as visible command-line arguments
Using delete commands without verifying the target project or task ID first
Assuming the CLI will prompt for confirmation before destructive operations (it will not)

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with both TickTick and Dida365?
faq.answer: Yes. TickTick and Dida365 are the same service. The API endpoint api.dida365.com works for both accounts.

faq.question: How do I get an OAuth token?
faq.answer: Visit the Dida365 Developer Center to register an OAuth app, then use the provided credentials with the auth flow or set TICKTICK_TOKEN directly.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. Claude Code can invoke the CLI script with appropriate arguments to manage tasks in your TickTick account.

faq.question: Is my token data safe?
faq.answer: Tokens are passed via environment variables. The skill does not store or transmit tokens to any third-party services beyond the official Dida365 API.

faq.question: Why is my request timing out?
faq.answer: Check your TICKTICK_TIMEOUT setting. The default is 30 seconds. Increase it with --timeout 60s for slower connections.

faq.question: How does this compare to the official web interface?
faq.answer: This CLI provides programmatic access for automation. The web interface is better for manual task management with rich editing features.
